urn:contentItem:6JHC-TNY3-RRMK-P3X1-00000-00Targeted News Service Tipoffs: Law Firm Representation2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:58:20ZLexisNexisTargeted News Service Tipoffs: Law Firm RepresentationTargeted News ServiceWASHINGTON

WASHINGTON, May 6 -- Law firms acted as legal advisors and otherwise represented businesses and public policy organizations in the following actions, as noted by those organizations in news announcements.

* * *

BCLP is advising ESCO Technologies Inc. on its proposed acquisition of Megger Group Limited, a global provider of testing, monitoring and analytics for electric power infrastructure, among other businesses, for approximately $2.35 billion. The transaction represents a significant step in ESCO's growth strategy, expanding its Utility Solutions Group and strengthening its global capabilities. ESCO Technologies is a global provider of highly engineered products and solutions serving diverse end-markets. It manufactures filtration and fluid control products, advanced composites, as well as signature and power management solutions for aviation, Navy and industrial customers. ESCO is an industry leader in designing and manufacturing RF test and measurement products and systems; and provides diagnostic instruments, software and services to industrial power users and the electric utility and renewable energy industries. The company is headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri, and operates offices and manufacturing facilities around the world. The BCLP deal team was led by Fred Bartelsmeyer (US M&A and Corporate Finance) and Andrew Hart (UK M&A and Corporate Finance), assisted by Chidima Atueyi (UK M&A and Corporate Finance). Securities and corporate governance aspects of the transaction were led by Rob Endicott, assisted by Brian Feezel.

BCLP advised Credit Agricole Nord de France and Bpifrance on an over EUR20 million finance structuring granted to Equisolar, aimed at supporting the construction of a portfolio of approximately 70 rooftop photovoltaic power plants installed on equestrian centres. Equisolar, is is a joint venture created in 2024 between Eiffel Transition Infrastructure and Concept Sol. These facilities will be primarily deployed on equestrian centres across Northern France, representing a total installed capacity of nearly 30 MWp. Once fully operational, the portfolio is expected to generate electricity equivalent to the consumption of approximately 6,000 households. The BCLP team advising the lenders included Partner David Blondel, Senior Associate Gwenael Fassot, and Associate Fabien Lambert, on financing aspects, and Partner Jean-Pierre Delvigne and Senior Associate Maryne Gouhier, on energy law aspects.

Gibson Dunn is acting as financing counsel to General Catalyst-backed Long Lake Management on its acquisition of American Express Global Business Travel, a leading software and services company for travel, expense, and meetings and events. The Gibson Dunn finance team is led by partner Doug Horowitz and includes partners Kristen Poole and Dean Masuda, of counsel Jason Sison, and associate Eric Olson.

Gibson Dunn advised Arlington Capital Partners and Arlington portfolio company Pond & Company on the acquisition of ENERCON. The firm's corporate team included partners Andrew Herman and Evan D'Amico and associates Michael Naclerio, Sydney Colopy, and Thomas Franck.

Gibson Dunn is advising HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc., a leading investor in sustainable infrastructure assets, on its agreement with Ameresco, Inc. to form a newly formed joint venture focused on biofuels called Neogenyx Fuels. The Gibson Dunn corporate team includes partners Daniel Alterbaum and Nick Politan, of counsel Jonathan Sapp, and associate Matthew Goldstein. Partner Matt Donnelly and associate Abram Dorrough are advising on tax aspects. Partner Lauren Traina is advising on real estate aspects. Partner Michael Murphy is advising on environmental aspects. Partner Tory Lauterbach is advising on energy regulatory aspects.

Gibson Dunn is advising S+S Industries, a leading provider of critical solutions and value-added services for data center infrastructure, on the strategic investment in S+S by funds managed by Cerberus Capital Management, L.P. The firm's corporate team is led by partner Michael Piazza and includes partners Jesse Myers and Tyler Cox and associate Andrew Abell. Partner Michael Cannon is advising on tax aspects.

Gibson Dunn is advising SLB on its $2 billion offering of Senior Notes. Our corporate team includes partners Andrew Fabens and Cynthia Mabry, of counsel Rodrigo Surcan, and associate Chad Kang.

Gibson Dunn represented Coeur Mining, Inc. (NYSE: CDE), a U.S.-based diverse precious metals producer, in its private exchange offer to eligible holders for any and all of the outstanding 6.875% Senior Notes due 2032 issued by New Gold Inc., in exchange for Coeur's 6.875% Senior Notes due 2032 and cash, in connection with Coeur's acquisition of New Gold. Coeur exchanged $385,800,000 aggregate principal amount of New Gold's Senior Notes (representing 96.45% of the outstanding New Gold Senior Notes) for $385,774,000 aggregate principal amount of new notes and approximately $771,600 in cash. The exchange offer settled on April 22, 2026. Our team included partner Eric Scarazzo, of counsel Rodrigo Surcan, and associate Mashoka Maimona. Partners Darius Mehraban and Andy Chen and associate Stephen Arvid Berg advised on finance aspects. Partner Kathryn Kelly and associate Eytan de Gunzburg advised on tax aspects.

Gibson Dunn represented Related Digital, a data center development and investment platform founded by our client Related Companies, in the formation of a joint venture with funds affiliated with Blackstone in connection with the development and financing of a $16 billion hyperscale data center campus in Saline Township, Michigan, purpose-built for Oracle. The transaction includes equity investments from Related Digital and Blackstone and a landmark fixed-rate, long term debt financing anchored by PIMCO-managed funds and accounts. The transaction closed on April 30, 2026. This transaction is another market-leading achievement for Gibson Dunn's Data Centers and Digital Infrastructure Practice Group which continues to be called upon for the largest data center transactions around the globe. The GDC team included partners Andrew Dady, Lauren Traina, David Perechocky, and Matthew Kidd; of counsel Elizabeth Case, Stephen Flug, William Goldman, and Genta Stafaj; and associates Matthew Schroth and Curtis Stahl. Partner Evan Gusler advised on tax matters.

Goodwin's Hong Kong Private Equity team led a cross-office team advising Templewater on its acquisition of Ascensus Health Group (AHG), a leading Singapore-based provider of specialist women's healthcare services. The acquisition marks a further step in Templewater's strategy to build an integrated women's healthcare platform across Asia. Founded by leading obstetrics and gynaecology medical professionals, AHG is a trusted provider of specialist women's health services, which operates a network of 13 clinics across Singapore and Malaysia. Its services include obstetrics and gynaecology, gynae-oncology, pediatrics, dermatology, and related healthcare services. The acquisition represents the second transaction Goodwin has completed this year for Templewater, following Goodwin's advice to Templewater on its acquisition of The Women's Clinic Group in March.

Goodwin advised Esperion Therapeutics ("Esperion") on entering into a definitive agreement with ARCHIMED, a leading investment firm focused exclusively on healthcare industries, under which funds managed by ARCHIMED will acquire Esperion in a transaction valued at up to approximately $1.1 billion in total equity value on a fully diluted basis, assuming full achievement of certain commercial-based milestones. Under the terms of the agreement, Esperion shareholders will receive $3.16 per share in cash at closing, plus the right to participate in contingent milestone payments of up to $100 million in the aggregate tied to future net sales performance. The upfront cash consideration represents a premium of 58% to Esperion's closing share price on April 30, 2026. The Goodwin team was led by Lisa Haddad, Jean Lee, Stuart Cable, Andrew Yu, Kaitlyn Buffaloe, Madeline Linder, Christopher Li, Michael Shuster, Duncan Greenhalgh, Noelle Dubiansky, Miho Kaneko, Gina Kim, Alex Denniston, James Devendorf, James Oh, Jennifer Fay, Lauren NuDelman, Julie Tibbets, Krupa Zachariah, Jackie Klosek, Curtis McCluskey, Jacob Lee, Arman Oruc, Ortal Ben-Aharon, Arom Yun, Heath Ingram, Nathan Brodeur, Dan Karelitz, Shlomo Gottesman, Kristopher Ring, Arthur Nahas, Liza Craig, Candi Alfred, Brian Mukherjee, Jacob Osborn, Gozde Guckaya, Carrie Clowney, James Barri, Mitchell Bloom, Justin Platt, Karin Yoo, Andrea Akinbola, and Angela Kim.

Goodwin has advised Gilde Healthcare Partners (Gilde) on the acquisition financing for Physio CKI GmbH (Physio), a leading provider of high-quality physical therapy services. The financing provides a strong foundation for Physio to continue executing its growth strategy and to further expand its network of clinics as part of its partnership with Gilde Healthcare. Physio is based in Cologne, North Rhine-Westphalia, and employs over 200 people. The company provides physiotherapy services of the highest standard in 13 practices in the greater Rhine-Sieg, Cologne-Bonn, and Dusseldorf areas, all of which feature a uniform, well-thought-out corporate design and create a high-quality atmosphere. Goodwin's transaction team consisted of Winfried M. Carli, Daniel Wagner, Rina Omura (all Private Equity / Debt Finance, Munich ) and Philipp Lauer ( Tax, Munich ).

Goodwin advised investors including Flying Fish, Evantic and BOND on their participation in the $1.1 billion investment round into Ineffable Intelligence, a new AI research lab in London, founded in November 2025, with a mission to make first contact with superintelligence. The investment round is reported to be the largest seed round ever raised in Europe. The company is building a superlearner; a system that discovers all knowledge directly through its own experience, from elementary motor skills to profound intellectual breakthroughs, with the potential to rediscover and then transcend the greatest inventions in human history. Flying Fish Ventures is a Seattle-based venture capital firm focused on pre-seed and seed-stage investments in artificial intelligence and machine learning startups, partnering with founders building AI-driven companies and providing both capital and strategic support. The Goodwin team advising investors including Flying Fish, Evantic and BOND was led by Andrew Davis, Emily Townsend, and Harriet Worthington. Goodwin also acted for other investors that participated in the investment round.

Hughes Hubbard is advising the Chapter 11 trustee of Kentucky Owl LLC, an affiliate of Stoli Group, in its restructuring proceedings. The firm guided the trustee in seeking court approval to hire an inventory broker to sell more than 35,000 barrels of aging bourbon in efforts to maximize creditor recoveries. Hughes Hubbard is advising the Chapter 11 trustee appointed to run the chapter 11 case filed in late 2024 by Kentucky Owl, a bourbon brand affiliated with Stoli Group. Recently, Hughes Hubbard assisted the trustee in securing the bankruptcy court's approval to hire an inventory broker to help sell more than 35,000 barrels of Kentucky Owl bourbon. In an application filed on April 20, Kentucky Owl's Chapter 11 trustee, Claudia Springer, asked Judge Scott W. Everett of the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the Northern District of Texas to approve the hiring of Time and Tasks as a broker to assist in monetizing its inventory, and in maximizing recoveries for creditors as the case moves toward liquidation. If approved, Time and Tasks would receive reduced commission rates to help market and sell Kentucky Owl's premium barrel inventory, which Springer described as time-sensitive and highly valuable due to aging and market demand.

Hughes Hubbard is advising the partners of Gleacher Shacklock, a premier independent advisory firm headquartered in London with a focus and long-standing presence in the UK advisory market, on the sale of their interests in Gleacher Shacklock to Perella Weinberg Partners, a leading global independent advisory firm. The transaction is expected to close in the second half of 2026, subject to regulatory approval and customary closing conditions. The Hughes Hubbard team is led by Gerold Niggemann and Javad Husain with assistance from Gary Simon, Chuck Samuelson and Erin DeCecchis. Hughes Hubbard is working closely with Gleacher Shacklock's General Counsel Chloe Gal and a team from Slaughter and May led by Robin Ogle and Warwick Brennand.

Kirkland & Ellis advised funds led by the Infrastructure Opportunities strategy of Ares Management Corporation on its acquisition from funds managed by Blackstone Energy Transition Partners of a 32.4% stake in the Rover Pipeline. Rover is a large-scale natural gas transmission pipeline that provides critical connectivity from the Appalachian Basin to high-value end markets across North America. The asset's footprint spans approximately 700 miles across Pennsylvania, West Virginia, Ohio and Michigan, with transportation capacity of 3.425 Bcf/d that is substantially contracted under long-term agreements with high-quality counterparties. Blackstone acquired its interest in Rover in 2017, supporting the pipeline's development and completion in 2018. The asset is operated by an affiliate of Energy Transfer LP, a leading North American midstream energy company. The Kirkland team included corporate lawyers Rob Goodin, Jimmy Chalk, Phil Greenfield, Aaron King, Tyler Ciavarra and Colby Gardner; tax lawyers Bill Dong and Griffin Peeples; debt finance lawyers Rohit Chaudhry, Olivia George and Meng Ding; energy regulatory lawyer Drew Stuyvenberg; environmental transactions lawyer Jim Dolphin; and real asset transactions lawyers Chad Smith and Jonathan Strom.

Kirkland & Ellis advised global investment firm KKR on a strategic investment to accelerate the growth of MLS NEXT Pro, MLS's professional development league. In connection, MLS and funds managed by KKR have formed Hometown Soccer Holdings ("HSH"), a new platform created to support the evolution of MLS NEXT Pro. Kirkland is also advising HSH. The partnership will serve as MLS NEXT Pro's commercial engine, supporting stadium development in new communities, creating new club brands for MLS-affiliated teams, and enhancing the fan experience through high-energy matchday environments at fan-friendly venues with distinct local identities. The investment will support the growth of the sport and the reach of MLS NEXT Pro. The Kirkland team included corporate lawyers Frank Saviano, Lee Blum, Jennifer Perkins and Alice Billmire; tax lawyers Adam Kool and Mike Beinus; technology & IP transactions lawyer Bernadette Coppola; and executive compensation lawyer Rohit Nafday.

Kirkland & Ellis advised Related Digital, a vertically integrated data center development and investment platform, in connection with the lease and financing arrangements supporting the development of a $16 billion hyperscale data center campus in Saline Township, Michigan. The financing, which closed today, includes equity investments from Related Digital and funds affiliated with Blackstone, together with fixed-rate, long-term debt financing anchored by PIMCO-managed funds and accounts. The Saline Township campus, known as "The Barn" -a reference to the historic red barn preserved at the project's entrance along Michigan Avenue -will comprise three single-story data center buildings with more than 1 GW of total capacity, positioning the development among the largest data center projects in North America. The project is being developed for Oracle as part of its partnership with OpenAI to expand AI compute capacity across the U.S. The Kirkland team advising on the lease included real estate lawyers Michael Shultz, Kim McGrath, Robin Basu, Max Baird and Katie Roddy. The Kirkland team advising on the financing included capital markets lawyers Julian Seiguer and Ieuan List; real estate lawyers Kim McGrath, Max Baird and Katie Roddy; and debt finance lawyers Mary Kogut and Will Zakhary.

Kirkland & Ellis advised TPG on the debt financing for its acquisition of Tamaki Health Group, New Zealand's largest independent primary healthcare provider. Tamaki Health Group operates more than 50 medical clinics across New Zealand, including the White Cross and Local Doctors brands, serving 350,000+ enrolled patients. The transaction closed in April 2026. The Kirkland team included debt finance lawyers Manas Chandrashekar, Brandon Ovington and Ryan Chan.

Kirkland & Ellis represented the placing agents, including BofA Securities, CICC, J.P. Morgan and Morgan Stanley (in alphabetical order), in connection with the H-share placing (the H-Share Placing) of Contemporary Amperex Technology Co., Limited (CATL, SZ:300750, HKEX:3750). The H-Share Placing was announced on April 28, 2026 and completed on 30 April, 2026. The net proceeds from the H-Share Placing (after deducting all fees, costs and expenses incurred by CATL) amounted to approximately HKD 39.12 billion (USD 5 billion), which will be used to accelerate the implementation of the global zerocarbon strategy and support long-term sustainable development. The Kirkland team included capital markets lawyers Mengyu Lu, Brian Ho, Justin Zhou, Ashlee Wu, Chuanwen Wang and Rock Liu; and government, regulatory & internal investigations lawyers Jodi Wu and Weng Keong Kok.

Paul Hastings (Europe) LLP acted as counsel on ESG and SFDR advisory work for TPG's The Rise Funds in their strategic investment in Zum Services Inc., a leading provider of school transport services. ESG & Sustainable Finance chair Ruth Knox led the Paul Hastings team, which also included associates Joanna Broadwith and Davis Woodruff.

Paul Hastings LLP advised an ad hoc group of secured lenders to PDS Holdco Inc. and its subsidiaries, doing business as Sonrava Health and Western Dental (Sonrava), in connection with a comprehensive, out-of-court recapitalization of Sonrava's $1.1 billion+ capital structure and a $100 million liquidity infusion. The transaction involved the implementation of a consensual restructuring supported by Sonrava, its former equity holders and Sonrava's lenders that reduced overall leverage, improved liquidity and positioned the business for sustainable growth. Financial Restructuring Group co-chair Jayme Goldstein led the team, which also included partners Matthew Schwartz, David Hong, Alex Cross, Jeremy Evans, Gary Silber and Isaac Sasson, of counsel Joanne Lau, and associates Kelsey Massey, Leonie Koch, Nilam Faqhir, Omar Abdelhamid and Alexis Fishman.

Paul Hastings LLP advised an ad hoc group of term lenders to Empire Today LLC and its subsidiaries (Empire Today) in connection with a comprehensive, out-of-court recapitalization of Empire Today's nearly $800 million capital structure and a $65 million new equity investment. The transaction involved the implementation of a fully consensual restructuring supported by the company and its lenders that reduced overall leverage, improved liquidity and positioned the business for sustainable growth. Financial Restructuring Group co-chair Jayme Goldstein and partner Isaac Sasson led the team, which also included partners Matthew Schwartz, Sal Perrotto, David Hong and Gary Silber, of counsel Joanne Lau and Tess Sadler, and associates Christine Geils, Leonie Koch, Omar Abdelhamid, Michael Reeves and Alexis Fishman.

Paul Hastings LLP advised Primary Wave Music, a strategic partner of Brookfield, on the formation of Primary Wave Music IP Fund 4, which held its final closing at $2.225 billion in total commitments, exceeding its original target of $1.5 billion and its hard cap of $2 billion. Backed by a diverse global investor base, including insurance companies, pension funds, sovereign wealth investors and others, Fund 4 is the largest dedicated closed-end music royalties fund in the industry to date. Paul Hastings also assisted in implementing a bespoke feeder fund for insurance capital and helping to establish a series of subscription line credit facilities for the fund complex. Investment Funds & Private Capital Partner Max Rosenberg led the Paul Hastings team, with associates Hailey Ullmann, Stanley Birch and Jake Bernstein. The team was supported by Ryan Swan and Zach Milloy (Funds Regulatory), Joseph Opich and Rob Wilson (Tax), Matthew Nemeth and Daniel Nicholas (Structured Credit), Lindsay Sparks (Finance) and Sidney Fohrman (Music).

Paul Hastings LLP advised STG, a private equity firm focused on software, data and analytics, in connection with the acquisition of Carrier Logistics Inc., the premier provider of transportation management software for less-than-truckload carriers. Partner Steve Camahort and associate Maggie Chou led the Paul Hastings team, which also included partners Michelle Reed, Lucas Rachuba, Todd Schneider and Dan Stellenberg, of counsel Talya Hutchison, Jennifer Riddle and Jeff Ii, and associates Tina Jeffcoat, Steph Lao, Nora Logsdon, Kelsey Massey, Raven Rowe, Elise Wu and Ajmal Zanher.

White & Case LLP has advised Arlington Capital Partners (Arlington), a Washington, DC-area based private investment firm specializing in government-regulated industries, on the financing of Arlington's acquisition of ENERCON and its subsidiaries (ENERCON), marking Arlington's largest-ever platform build. ENERCON, headquartered in Kennesaw, Georgia, delivers highly specialized nuclear engineering and licensing support and power-generating capabilities to commercial and federal customers. As part of the transaction, ENERCON will merge with Arlington portfolio company Pond & Company (POND), a full-service design and engineering firm providing regulatory-driven, highly technical services to the federal government and customers across the energy industry. White & Case previously advised Arlington on the financing of their acquisition of POND. The White & Case team was led by partner Brett Pallin and included associates Ryan Talbott and Nick Townsend (all in New York). Law clerk Gabrielle Sanchez (New York) also assisted on the matter.

White & Case LLP has advised Bregal Milestone on its majority growth investment in CoreGo Oy (CoreGo), a leading provider of open-loop payments and integrated technology solutions for festivals, sports events and venues across Europe. The transaction represents a significant investment by Bregal Milestone in the fast-growing payment technology sector and will support CoreGo's continued growth and international expansion. The White & Case team which advised on the transaction was led by partner Victor Aberg (Helsinki) and included partner Marika Harjula (Brussels & Helsinki) and associates Roosa Vare, Oona Keskinen, Arvi Saarinen, Willie Zhang and Joona Krigsman (all Helsinki).

White & Case LLP has advised certain heirs of Martha Adrianna Nathan, born Dreyfus (1874 - 1958), on proceedings before the Independent Commission SKKG ("the Commission") concerning the painting "Thunersee mit Blu u mlisalp und Niesen" (1882) by Ferdinand Hodler, which has been in the Stiftung fur Kunst, Kultur und Geschichte (SKKG) collection since 1998. The White & Case team which advised on this matter was led by partners Brendan Quinn (Melbourne) and Jasper Wauters (Geneva), and included counsel Igor Golshtein and Chris Skordas (both Melbourne), associates Nikolas Hertel (Geneva), Andrew Evans, Elloise Campbell, Mili Simonovich and trainee Claire Qian (all Melbourne).

White & Case LLP has advised NRG Energy, Inc. (NYSE: NRG), a leading North American energy and home services company, on its offering of US$500 million of senior secured first-lien notes and US$2.1 billion of senior unsecured notes, and its new US$900 million term loan B facility. The proceeds of the financings will be used to repay a portion of the outstanding borrowings under NRG's revolving credit facility, repurchase and redeem in full the outstanding secured notes of the company's wholly owned subsidiary, Lightning Power, LLC, to pay transaction costs, and for general corporate purposes. The White & Case team was led by Capital Markets partners Rafael Roberti, Eliza McDougall and Yehuda Rubel, and included associates Elizabeth Mapelli, Susie Qu, Ashley Chana, Thomas Boulger and Sonia Patel (all in New York). Law clerk Chantal Isaac (New York) also assisted on the matter.

White & Case LLP advised Pale Fire Capital, a prominent Czech-based investment firm, and founders including Petr Samek, on the disposal of ROUVY, a leading indoor cycling and virtual training platform built on real-world routes, to California-based private company and leading global digital fitness platform Zwift. The transaction demonstrates the strong business potential of virtual indoor cycling platforms. The business offers exposure to a high-growth segment where user engagement and recurring revenues have proven resilient even in uncertain macroeconomic environments. As a technology-focused investor, Pale Fire Capital has played a key role in supporting the app's digital innovation and its scaling to reach a global user base. The White & Case team which advised on the transaction was co-led by partners Caitlin Powell Gimpel (Milan &New York) and Petr Panek (Prague) and local partner Jana Chwaszcz (Prague) and included partners Vaclav Zaloudek (Prague), David Dreier, Elodie Gal (both New York), Heather Greenfield (Washington, DC) and Samantha Rozell (Chicago), local partner Magda Olysarova (Prague), counsel Isaac Tendler (New York) and Jakub Mencl (Prague) and associates Barbora Vaculova, Pavel Hrdy, Barbora Smekalova (all Prague), Sunrita Sen (Chicago), Mark Kim and Jacob Smith (both New York) and tax consultant Milan Horak (Prague).

White & Case LLP has advised Solaria Energia y Medio Ambiente (Solaria) on its EUR300 million accelerated bookbuild. The funds will help transform Solaria's strategy, evolving from a leading independent solar power producer in Europe into a multi-technology renewable energy platform and Data Center Powered Land (DCP Land) business, with the ambition of reaching 9 GW of total installed capacity by 2028. They will be used to deploy 3.4 GW of secured capacity in five countries and increase growth in hybrid battery energy storage systems (BESS) and wind, where Solaria aims to invest EUR770 million of equity to hybridize its solar parks with wind and battery storage solutions. The transaction represents 10 percent of Solaria's share capital and was met with strong demand from high quality institutional investors globally, with the order book covered 6.7 times across the price range and attracting broad international participation, including long-only funds, infrastructure investors and specialist energy accounts. The White & Case team in which advised on the transaction was led by partner Javier Monzon and local partner Lucia Quiroga (both Madrid) and included partners Inigo Esteve and Laura Sizemore (both London), local partner Julia Villalon (Madrid), associates Victoria Paternina (Madrid) and George Davie (London) and contract lawyer Bob Lehner (New York).

White & Case LLP has advised Sturm, Ruger & Company, Inc. (Ruger) with the May 2, 2026 resolution of a proxy contest initiated by Beretta Holding S.A. (Beretta Holding) with Ruger's 2026 annual meeting. Further information is available on Ruger's Form 8-K (available here ) and in the press release (available here ). The White & Case team was led by M&A partners Richard Brand and Kiran Kadekar, counsel Patti Marks and associate Edward Ernst (all in New York), with valuable contributions from M&A partner Keith Hallam and associates Kristie Chan, Gabrielle Sanchez, Jean Song (all in New York) and Katelyn Meylor (Silicone Valley). CFIUS-related matters were led by partners Farhad Jalinous and Ryan Brady and associate Wes Hutson (all in Washington, DC). Global antitrust and competition matters were led by partners Rebecca Farrington (Washington, DC) and Michael Engel (London) and associate Gabriela Baca (Washington, DC). Dispute resolution-related matters were led by partners David Hille and Andrew Hammond and associate Romer Telemi (all in New York).

Known for its emphasis on renewable energy, Evergy drives sustainable growth through diverse clean energy initiatives. Evergy's competitive strengths include its scale and technology leadership in integrating renewable sources. Through programs like the Evergy Good Hometown Grant, the company underscores its commitment to corporate social responsibility, supporting Kansas nonprofits and enhancing community engagement.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Evergy Inc., headquartered in Kansas, is a prominent electric utility company serving approximately 1.6 million customers across Kansas and Missouri. Its core business centers on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity through a diversified energy portfolio that includes coal, natural gas, nuclear, and renewable sources. The company plays a vital role in regional energy reliability and sustainability, with a strong emphasis on modernizing its infrastructure to enhance efficiency and reduce emissions. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic focus positions Evergy to capitalize on the transition to a low-carbon economy, supporting long-term growth and innovation in the utility sector. It is the NASDAQ's largest Publishing company by market capitalisation.

In a strategic move towards advancing clean energy solutions, the Evergy TerraPower Kansas nuclear plant proposal marks a significant milestone in the Evergy TerraPower partnership. This collaboration focuses on the Evergy Kansas nuclear project, which will be powered by an Evergy Natrium reactor. The TerraPower Evergy collaboration is poised to enhance Evergy's nuclear power capabilities, with a comprehensive Evergy nuclear feasibility study in progress. Amidst Evergy EVRG stock news, this venture could affect Evergy EVRG stock, reflective of the company's robust infrastructure investments and Evergy grid modernization efforts. Key to Evergy power generation Kansas, the company is also expanding its portfolio with Evergy renewable energy projects, including Evergy wind energy programs and Evergy solar energy programs, reinforcing Evergy sustainability initiatives. As Evergy develops commercial energy solutions and optimizes residential electricity plans, Evergy electricity rates Kansas remain competitive. With ongoing Evergy smart meter programs and Evergy net metering Kansas facilities, Evergy's energy efficiency programs reflect their dedication to modernization and customer satisfaction. The Evergy nuclear partnership announcement, backed by Evergy CEO TerraPower statements, reiterates Evergy's commitment to innovative power plant development and comprehensive Evergy project permitting Kansas processes.

Thu Apr 9, 2026: Evergy Inc Reaches All-Time High as Investors Show Confidence

3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 80 words

Evergy Forges Advanced Nuclear Partnership With TerraPower and Kansas

Evergy, in partnership with TerraPower and the Kansas Department of Commerce, signed a memorandum of understanding to explore siting a Natrium advanced nuclear reactor in Kansas. The project, backed by significant private financing, aims to add 345-500 MW of carbon-free baseload generation to meet growing industrial demand. While the agreement positions Evergy as a leader in next-generation nuclear technology, it faces substantial risks related to regulatory approval, construction costs, and public acceptance, with a project timeline likely extending beyond 2030.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 71 words

Evergy Unveils $17.5 Billion Capital Investment Program Through 2029

Evergy announced a $17.5 billion five-year capital investment plan to accommodate unprecedented demand growth, largely from data centers. The program allocates funds for new natural gas and solar generation, transmission upgrades, and grid modernization, targeting an 8.5% annualized rate base growth. The plan will be financed through cash flow, debt, and equity, which may dilute existing shares. Execution depends on managing risks like cost inflation, supply chain issues, and regulatory approvals.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 69 words

Kansas Regulators Approve $128 Million Rate Increase Settlement

The Kansas Corporation Commission approved a $128 million annual revenue increase for Evergy Kansas Central, a significant reduction from the initial $196.4 million request. The settlement, reached with multiple stakeholders, balances infrastructure investment needs with customer affordability, resulting in an average monthly bill increase of $8.47 for residential customers. This outcome provides regulatory clarity and facilitates cost recovery for grid modernization projects, demonstrating a constructive but disciplined regulatory environment.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 68 words

Regulators Approve Landmark Tariffs for Data Center Power Supply

Regulators in Missouri and Kansas approved specialized tariffs for large-load customers like data centers, removing a key barrier to securing contracts for new projects. The tariffs establish rates 7-10% above existing industrial prices, require long-term commitments, and hold customers responsible for their interconnection costs. The structure ensures existing ratepayers are not subsidizing new growth and includes clean energy options, making Evergy's service area more attractive for hyperscale development.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 66 words

Evergy Reports Q3 Results, Raises Quarterly Dividend 4 Percent

Evergy reported third-quarter GAAP earnings of $2.03 per share and announced a 4% quarterly dividend increase to $0.6950 per share. Despite 2% weather-normalized demand growth, mild weather conditions led management to narrow its full-year 2025 EPS guidance to $3.92-$4.02. The results reflect the conflict between strong underlying demand and headwinds from interest expenses and regulatory lag, while the dividend hike signals confidence in future cash flow.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

Evergy Secures Regulatory Approval for 2,184 MW Generation Expansion

Kansas and Missouri regulators approved Evergy's plans to add 2,184 MW of new generation capacity, including two combined-cycle gas plants, one simple-cycle gas plant, and a solar facility. These projects, slated for operation between 2026 and 2030, are designed to meet projected load growth and facilitate the retirement of coal plants. The approvals provide regulatory certainty for construction, though the company faces risks related to cost inflation, fuel price volatility, and supply chain constraints.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

Lambda Announces $500 Million AI Infrastructure Investment in Evergy Territory

AI cloud provider Lambda announced a $500 million investment to develop an AI Factory in Kansas City, Missouri, repurposing a dormant data center. The facility will launch in 2026 with 24 MW of capacity, scaling beyond 100 MW and featuring high-density NVIDIA GPUs. This project validates Evergy's strategy to attract large-load customers and strengthens the case for its significant capital investments, diversifying its data center client base beyond existing commitments from Meta and Google.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 71 words

Evergy Economic Development Pipeline Surges Past 15 Gigawatts

Evergy's economic development pipeline has grown to over 15 GW, with 4-6 GW of high-probability large-load opportunities, primarily from the data center sector. This potential demand, which could double the utility's current peak load, is the driving force behind its $17.5 billion capital program. While the pipeline validates the company's growth strategy, significant execution risk remains, as converting these opportunities into energized loads depends on navigating complex development and regulatory stages.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 65 words

Evergy Deploys Advanced eSIM Technology to Enhance Grid Resilience

Evergy is deploying Kigen's eSIM technology to improve grid resilience by enabling secure, automated failover between its private LTE network and public cellular networks. This cybersecurity-focused initiative supports grid modernization by ensuring continuous connectivity for smart meters and IoT devices. The technology enhances reliability and supports advanced applications like dynamic billing, representing a foundational investment in long-term operational capability rather than a near-term earnings driver.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 67 words

Edison Electric Institute Recognizes Evergy for Emergency Response Excellence

Evergy received a national Emergency Response Award from the Edison Electric Institute for its outstanding restoration efforts during a Memorial Day storm and its mutual assistance to AEP Kentucky after Hurricane Helene. The award recognizes operational excellence in restoring power, coordinating resources, and ensuring safety. While the recognition has limited direct financial impact, it validates the utility's operational competence and strengthens its reputation with regulators and customers.

Apr 22, 2026: Anterix Closes Benton PUD Private Wireless Sale, Expanding Utility Private-LTE Adoption Base Relevant to Evergy's Grid Modernization Plan

On April 21, 2026, Anterix Inc., through PDV Spectrum Holding Company, LLC, entered into a spectrum license sale agreement with Public Utility District No. 1 of Benton County for a 10 MHz 900 MHz broadband license in Washington state, valued at $771,000. According to industry reports, the utility-owned network will serve more than 58,000 customers across a 927-square-mile territory.

Market data indicates the transaction represents the first private wireless broadband deployment among publicly owned utilities in the Pacific Northwest. The deal expands the national footprint of 900 MHz private-LTE utility networks.

Impact on Evergy: EVRG

Following this development, Evergy gains further industry evidence supporting its existing Anterix 900 MHz spectrum agreement and private LTE deployment strategy across Kansas and Missouri. Operational metrics indicate Evergy serves approximately 1.7 million customers and has identified private wireless as foundational to its Sustainability Transformation Plan, which includes advanced metering, distribution automation, and distributed energy resource integration.

Analysis shows the Benton PUD agreement adds another deployed utility to the 900 MHz ecosystem, reinforcing vendor scale and standardized integration practices relevant to Evergy's rollout. The growing deployment base also strengthens the regulatory record before Kansas and Missouri commissions overseeing capital recovery.

ECO NEWS 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 414 words

Apr 14, 2026: Oracle Names Evergy as Customer in AI Utilities Suite Launch, Positioning the Midwest Utility for Early Adoption of GenAI Operational Tools

Oracle Corporation unveiled a comprehensive expansion of its Utilities Industry Suite on April 13, 2026, at the Oracle Customer Edge Summit in Austin, Texas, introducing artificial intelligence and generative AI capabilities designed to modernize utility operations at scale. New tools include GenAI Asset Summarization, AI-powered meter data management, customer affordability solutions, and advanced grid and Distributed Energy Resource management. Oracle's Opower platform, which serves 45 million North American households and has delivered $4.3 billion in cumulative energy bill savings, anchors the expanded offering.

The launch reflects Oracle's strategy of embedding large language model capabilities directly into sector-specific utility workflows, positioning the company as a vertically integrated provider across customer engagement, operational intelligence, asset management, and grid infrastructure. Oracle's identification of specific customer references at the Customer Edge Summit signals the commercial maturity of its expanded platform and highlights utilities that have already committed to adoption of next-generation AI-powered operational tools.

The inclusion of named customers in the April 2026 announcement reflects Oracle's broader commercial strategy of using early adopters as reference deployments to accelerate platform evaluation among peer utilities. Mid-sized regulated utilities with active renewable energy integration programs and aging infrastructure asset management requirements represent a core segment of Oracle's target market for the expanded suite.

Impact on Evergy: EVRG

Following this news, Evergy - identified as a named Oracle customer in the April 2026 announcement - is among the first utilities positioned to adopt and operationalize newly launched AI tools, including GenAI Asset Summarization and AI-powered meter data management. For a mid-sized regulated utility serving Kansas and Missouri, these capabilities address practical operational challenges including aging infrastructure asset management and DER integration as the company expands its renewable portfolio.

Evergy's named inclusion also reflects its role as a reference customer, which may influence procurement decisions among peer utilities evaluating Oracle's expanded platform. This positioning reinforces Evergy's technology modernization posture with state regulators in Kansas and Missouri, where demonstrating commitment to operational innovation may support regulatory relationships and rate case positioning.

The operational relevance of Oracle's expanded suite to Evergy's specific challenges - aging transmission and distribution infrastructure, growing renewable energy integration, and regulatory compliance requirements - reflects a well-aligned deployment context. Evergy's early adoption status positions it to realize operational benefits from the new AI tools ahead of peer utilities while contributing deployment experience that may inform Oracle's broader utility market rollout.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Evergy Inc., headquartered in Kansas, is a prominent electric utility company serving approximately 1.6 million customers across Kansas and Missouri. Its core business centers on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity through a diversified energy portfolio that includes coal, natural gas, nuclear, and renewable sources. The company plays a vital role in regional energy reliability and sustainability, with a strong emphasis on modernizing its infrastructure to enhance efficiency and reduce emissions. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic focus positions Evergy to capitalize on the transition to a low-carbon economy, supporting long-term growth and innovation in the utility sector. It is the NASDAQ's largest Publishing company by market capitalisation.

In a strategic move towards advancing clean energy solutions, the Evergy TerraPower Kansas nuclear plant proposal marks a significant milestone in the Evergy TerraPower partnership. This collaboration focuses on the Evergy Kansas nuclear project, which will be powered by an Evergy Natrium reactor. The TerraPower Evergy collaboration is poised to enhance Evergy's nuclear power capabilities, with a comprehensive Evergy nuclear feasibility study in progress. Amidst Evergy EVRG stock news, this venture could affect Evergy EVRG stock, reflective of the company's robust infrastructure investments and Evergy grid modernization efforts. Key to Evergy power generation Kansas, the company is also expanding its portfolio with Evergy renewable energy projects, including Evergy wind energy programs and Evergy solar energy programs, reinforcing Evergy sustainability initiatives. As Evergy develops commercial energy solutions and optimizes residential electricity plans, Evergy electricity rates Kansas remain competitive. With ongoing Evergy smart meter programs and Evergy net metering Kansas facilities, Evergy's energy efficiency programs reflect their dedication to modernization and customer satisfaction. The Evergy nuclear partnership announcement, backed by Evergy CEO TerraPower statements, reiterates Evergy's commitment to innovative power plant development and comprehensive Evergy project permitting Kansas processes.

3.2 PAST YEAR TOP 10 STORIES

Story 1 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 80 words

Evergy Forges Advanced Nuclear Partnership With TerraPower and Kansas

Evergy, in partnership with TerraPower and the Kansas Department of Commerce, signed a memorandum of understanding to explore siting a Natrium advanced nuclear reactor in Kansas. The project, backed by significant private financing, aims to add 345-500 MW of carbon-free baseload generation to meet growing industrial demand. While the agreement positions Evergy as a leader in next-generation nuclear technology, it faces substantial risks related to regulatory approval, construction costs, and public acceptance, with a project timeline likely extending beyond 2030.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 71 words

Evergy Unveils $17.5 Billion Capital Investment Program Through 2029

Evergy announced a $17.5 billion five-year capital investment plan to accommodate unprecedented demand growth, largely from data centers. The program allocates funds for new natural gas and solar generation, transmission upgrades, and grid modernization, targeting an 8.5% annualized rate base growth. The plan will be financed through cash flow, debt, and equity, which may dilute existing shares. Execution depends on managing risks like cost inflation, supply chain issues, and regulatory approvals.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 69 words

Kansas Regulators Approve $128 Million Rate Increase Settlement

The Kansas Corporation Commission approved a $128 million annual revenue increase for Evergy Kansas Central, a significant reduction from the initial $196.4 million request. The settlement, reached with multiple stakeholders, balances infrastructure investment needs with customer affordability, resulting in an average monthly bill increase of $8.47 for residential customers. This outcome provides regulatory clarity and facilitates cost recovery for grid modernization projects, demonstrating a constructive but disciplined regulatory environment.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 68 words

Regulators Approve Landmark Tariffs for Data Center Power Supply

Regulators in Missouri and Kansas approved specialized tariffs for large-load customers like data centers, removing a key barrier to securing contracts for new projects. The tariffs establish rates 7-10% above existing industrial prices, require long-term commitments, and hold customers responsible for their interconnection costs. The structure ensures existing ratepayers are not subsidizing new growth and includes clean energy options, making Evergy's service area more attractive for hyperscale development.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 66 words

Evergy Reports Q3 Results, Raises Quarterly Dividend 4 Percent

Evergy reported third-quarter GAAP earnings of $2.03 per share and announced a 4% quarterly dividend increase to $0.6950 per share. Despite 2% weather-normalized demand growth, mild weather conditions led management to narrow its full-year 2025 EPS guidance to $3.92-$4.02. The results reflect the conflict between strong underlying demand and headwinds from interest expenses and regulatory lag, while the dividend hike signals confidence in future cash flow.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

Evergy Secures Regulatory Approval for 2,184 MW Generation Expansion

Kansas and Missouri regulators approved Evergy's plans to add 2,184 MW of new generation capacity, including two combined-cycle gas plants, one simple-cycle gas plant, and a solar facility. These projects, slated for operation between 2026 and 2030, are designed to meet projected load growth and facilitate the retirement of coal plants. The approvals provide regulatory certainty for construction, though the company faces risks related to cost inflation, fuel price volatility, and supply chain constraints.

Story 7 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

Lambda Announces $500 Million AI Infrastructure Investment in Evergy Territory

AI cloud provider Lambda announced a $500 million investment to develop an AI Factory in Kansas City, Missouri, repurposing a dormant data center. The facility will launch in 2026 with 24 MW of capacity, scaling beyond 100 MW and featuring high-density NVIDIA GPUs. This project validates Evergy's strategy to attract large-load customers and strengthens the case for its significant capital investments, diversifying its data center client base beyond existing commitments from Meta and Google.

Story 8 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 71 words

Evergy Economic Development Pipeline Surges Past 15 Gigawatts

Evergy's economic development pipeline has grown to over 15 GW, with 4-6 GW of high-probability large-load opportunities, primarily from the data center sector. This potential demand, which could double the utility's current peak load, is the driving force behind its $17.5 billion capital program. While the pipeline validates the company's growth strategy, significant execution risk remains, as converting these opportunities into energized loads depends on navigating complex development and regulatory stages.

Story 9 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 65 words

Evergy Deploys Advanced eSIM Technology to Enhance Grid Resilience

Evergy is deploying Kigen's eSIM technology to improve grid resilience by enabling secure, automated failover between its private LTE network and public cellular networks. This cybersecurity-focused initiative supports grid modernization by ensuring continuous connectivity for smart meters and IoT devices. The technology enhances reliability and supports advanced applications like dynamic billing, representing a foundational investment in long-term operational capability rather than a near-term earnings driver.

Story 10 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 67 words

Edison Electric Institute Recognizes Evergy for Emergency Response Excellence

Evergy received a national Emergency Response Award from the Edison Electric Institute for its outstanding restoration efforts during a Memorial Day storm and its mutual assistance to AEP Kentucky after Hurricane Helene. The award recognizes operational excellence in restoring power, coordinating resources, and ensuring safety. While the recognition has limited direct financial impact, it validates the utility's operational competence and strengthens its reputation with regulators and customers.

3.3 ECONOMIC NEWS

ECO NEWS 1 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 213 words

Apr 22, 2026: Anterix Closes Benton PUD Private Wireless Sale, Expanding Utility Private-LTE Adoption Base Relevant to Evergy's Grid Modernization Plan

On April 21, 2026, Anterix Inc., through PDV Spectrum Holding Company, LLC, entered into a spectrum license sale agreement with Public Utility District No. 1 of Benton County for a 10 MHz 900 MHz broadband license in Washington state, valued at $771,000. According to industry reports, the utility-owned network will serve more than 58,000 customers across a 927-square-mile territory.

Market data indicates the transaction represents the first private wireless broadband deployment among publicly owned utilities in the Pacific Northwest. The deal expands the national footprint of 900 MHz private-LTE utility networks.

Impact on Evergy: EVRG

Following this development, Evergy gains further industry evidence supporting its existing Anterix 900 MHz spectrum agreement and private LTE deployment strategy across Kansas and Missouri. Operational metrics indicate Evergy serves approximately 1.7 million customers and has identified private wireless as foundational to its Sustainability Transformation Plan, which includes advanced metering, distribution automation, and distributed energy resource integration.

Analysis shows the Benton PUD agreement adds another deployed utility to the 900 MHz ecosystem, reinforcing vendor scale and standardized integration practices relevant to Evergy's rollout. The growing deployment base also strengthens the regulatory record before Kansas and Missouri commissions overseeing capital recovery.

ECO NEWS 2 of 2, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 414 words

Apr 14, 2026: Oracle Names Evergy as Customer in AI Utilities Suite Launch, Positioning the Midwest Utility for Early Adoption of GenAI Operational Tools

Oracle Corporation unveiled a comprehensive expansion of its Utilities Industry Suite on April 13, 2026, at the Oracle Customer Edge Summit in Austin, Texas, introducing artificial intelligence and generative AI capabilities designed to modernize utility operations at scale. New tools include GenAI Asset Summarization, AI-powered meter data management, customer affordability solutions, and advanced grid and Distributed Energy Resource management. Oracle's Opower platform, which serves 45 million North American households and has delivered $4.3 billion in cumulative energy bill savings, anchors the expanded offering.

The launch reflects Oracle's strategy of embedding large language model capabilities directly into sector-specific utility workflows, positioning the company as a vertically integrated provider across customer engagement, operational intelligence, asset management, and grid infrastructure. Oracle's identification of specific customer references at the Customer Edge Summit signals the commercial maturity of its expanded platform and highlights utilities that have already committed to adoption of next-generation AI-powered operational tools.

The inclusion of named customers in the April 2026 announcement reflects Oracle's broader commercial strategy of using early adopters as reference deployments to accelerate platform evaluation among peer utilities. Mid-sized regulated utilities with active renewable energy integration programs and aging infrastructure asset management requirements represent a core segment of Oracle's target market for the expanded suite.

Impact on Evergy: EVRG

Following this news, Evergy - identified as a named Oracle customer in the April 2026 announcement - is among the first utilities positioned to adopt and operationalize newly launched AI tools, including GenAI Asset Summarization and AI-powered meter data management. For a mid-sized regulated utility serving Kansas and Missouri, these capabilities address practical operational challenges including aging infrastructure asset management and DER integration as the company expands its renewable portfolio.

Evergy's named inclusion also reflects its role as a reference customer, which may influence procurement decisions among peer utilities evaluating Oracle's expanded platform. This positioning reinforces Evergy's technology modernization posture with state regulators in Kansas and Missouri, where demonstrating commitment to operational innovation may support regulatory relationships and rate case positioning.

The operational relevance of Oracle's expanded suite to Evergy's specific challenges - aging transmission and distribution infrastructure, growing renewable energy integration, and regulatory compliance requirements - reflects a well-aligned deployment context. Evergy's early adoption status positions it to realize operational benefits from the new AI tools ahead of peer utilities while contributing deployment experience that may inform Oracle's broader utility market rollout.

urn:contentItem:6JGC-7Y53-S40W-720N-00000-00Evergy Inc (NASDAQ:EVRG) offers dividend yield of 3.3%; -27c [-0.3%]2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:51:20ZLexisNexisEvergy Inc (NASDAQ:EVRG) offers dividend yield of 3.3%; -27c [-0.3%]

NASDAQ END-OF-DAY STOCK REPORT

www.buysellsignals.com

Evergy Inc (NASDAQ: EVRG $82.57) ranks 2 in the Top 20 by dividend yielding stocks in the S&P 500 Index of 159 stocks.

Evergy Inc. (NASDAQ:EVRG) operates as a leading electricity provider in Kansas, rooted in a robust power utility ecosystem. Known for its emphasis on renewable energy, Evergy drives sustainable growth through diverse clean energy initiatives. Strategically positioning itself in the energy sector, the company has announced a partnership with TerraPower on a Kansas nuclear power project, highlighting its innovation pipeline. Evergy Inc., headquartered in Kansas, is a prominent electric utility company serving approximately 1.6 million customers across Kansas and Missouri. The company plays a vital role in regional energy reliability and sustainability, with a strong emphasis on modernizing its infrastructure to enhance efficiency and reduce emissions. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, particularly wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to increasing demand for clean power and supportive regulatory policies. Key to Evergy power generation Kansas, the company is also expanding its portfolio with Evergy renewable energy projects, including Evergy wind energy programs and Evergy solar energy programs, reinforcing Evergy sustainability initiatives. As Evergy develops commercial energy solutions and optimizes residential electricity plans, Evergy electricity rates Kansas remain competitive. Evergy Forges Advanced Nuclear Partnership With TerraPower and Kansas

Evergy, in partnership with TerraPower and the Kansas Department of Commerce, signed a memorandum of understanding to explore siting a Natrium advanced nuclear reactor in Kansas. The project, backed by significant private financing, aims to add 345-500 MW of carbon-free baseload generation to meet growing industrial demand. While the agreement positions Evergy as a leader in next-generation nuclear technology, it faces substantial risks related to regulatory approval, construction costs, and public acceptance, with a project timeline likely extending beyond 2030.

Story 2 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 71 words

Evergy Unveils $17.5 Billion Capital Investment Program Through 2029

Evergy announced a $17.5 billion five-year capital investment plan to accommodate unprecedented demand growth, largely from data centers. The program allocates funds for new natural gas and solar generation, transmission upgrades, and grid modernization, targeting an 8.5% annualized rate base growth. The plan will be financed through cash flow, debt, and equity, which may dilute existing shares. Execution depends on managing risks like cost inflation, supply chain issues, and regulatory approvals.

Story 3 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 69 words

Kansas Regulators Approve $128 Million Rate Increase Settlement

The Kansas Corporation Commission approved a $128 million annual revenue increase for Evergy Kansas Central, a significant reduction from the initial $196.4 million request. The settlement, reached with multiple stakeholders, balances infrastructure investment needs with customer affordability, resulting in an average monthly bill increase of $8.47 for residential customers. This outcome provides regulatory clarity and facilitates cost recovery for grid modernization projects, demonstrating a constructive but disciplined regulatory environment.

Story 4 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 68 words

Regulators Approve Landmark Tariffs for Data Center Power Supply

Regulators in Missouri and Kansas approved specialized tariffs for large-load customers like data centers, removing a key barrier to securing contracts for new projects. The tariffs establish rates 7-10% above existing industrial prices, require long-term commitments, and hold customers responsible for their interconnection costs. The structure ensures existing ratepayers are not subsidizing new growth and includes clean energy options, making Evergy's service area more attractive for hyperscale development.

Story 5 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 66 words

Evergy Reports Q3 Results, Raises Quarterly Dividend 4 Percent

Evergy reported third-quarter GAAP earnings of $2.03 per share and announced a 4% quarterly dividend increase to $0.6950 per share. Despite 2% weather-normalized demand growth, mild weather conditions led management to narrow its full-year 2025 EPS guidance to $3.92-$4.02. The results reflect the conflict between strong underlying demand and headwinds from interest expenses and regulatory lag, while the dividend hike signals confidence in future cash flow.

Story 6 of 10, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 74 words

Evergy Secures Regulatory Approval for 2,184 MW Generation Expansion

Kansas and Missouri regulators approved Evergy's plans to add 2,184 MW of new generation capacity, including two combined-cycle gas plants, one simple-cycle gas plant, and a solar facility. These projects, slated for operation between 2026 and 2030, are designed to meet projected load growth and facilitate the retirement of coal plants. The approvals provide regulatory certainty for construction, though the company faces risks related to cost inflation, fuel price volatility, and supply chain constraints.

AI cloud provider Lambda announced a $500 million investment to develop an AI Factory in Kansas City, Missouri, repurposing a dormant data center. The facility will launch in 2026 with 24 MW of capacity, scaling beyond 100 MW and featuring high-density NVIDIA GPUs. This project validates Evergy's strategy to attract large-load customers and strengthens the case for its significant capital investments, diversifying its data center client base beyond existing commitments from Meta and Google.

While the pipeline validates the company's growth strategy, significant execution risk remains, as converting these opportunities into energized loads depends on navigating complex development and regulatory stages.

Evergy is deploying Kigen's eSIM technology to improve grid resilience by enabling secure, automated failover between its private LTE network and public cellular networks. Washington: Hydro Green Energy, LLC has submitted the following document to the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission:

Hydro Green Energy, LLC • 2901 4th Avenue South, # B253 • Birmingham, AL 35233www.hgenergy.comMEMORANDUM To: FERC Project Docket, P-15257 From: Hydro Green Energy – Project Development Date: May 1, 2026 Subject: Non-Delivery of mailed PADUnited State Postal Service Returned PAD ’ s sent to:Federal Energy Regulatory CommissionRegional EngineerDivision of Dam Safety and Inspections230 South Dearborn Street, Room 3130Chiago, IL 60604Federal Emergency Management AgencyRegional Administrator9221 West Parkway, Suite 300Kansas City, MO 64114-3372Department of Interior, Bureau of Land Management returned the PAD sent to:Bureau of Land ManagementUses and Hydropower Program LeadLands, Realty, and Cadastral Survey4701 N. Torrey Pines DriveNorth Las Vegas, NV 89130Sincerely,Wayne KrouseManaging Member,Lock+™ Hydro Friends Fund X, LLCChairman & CEOHydro Green Energy, LLC

The complete document can be viewed at this link: https://elibrary.ferc.gov

value.Title: Utilities in Focus: 3 Stocks That Could Lead This Earnings Cycle

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH6-P8N3-RRTG-82HJ-00000-00Utilities in Focus: 3 Stocks That Could Lead This Earnings Cycle2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:50:56ZLexisNexisUtilities in Focus: 3 Stocks That Could Lead This Earnings Cycle

The views expressed in any and all content distributed by Newstex and its re-distributors (collectively, the "Newstex Authoritative Content") are solely those of the respective author(s) and not necessarily the views of Newstex or its re-distributors. The Newstex Authoritative Content shall be construed as author-based content and commentary. Accordingly, no warranties or other guarantees are offered as to the quality of the opinions, commentary or anything else appearing in such Newstex Authoritative Content. Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

May 5th, 2026 (Zacks Investment Research — Delivered by Newstex)

The Zacks Utilities sector's first-quarter 2026 earnings are likely to have benefited from recently implemented electric, natural gas and water rate hikes, along with ongoing cost-efficiency measures and a growing customer base. Rising demand from data centers is also expected to have supported bottom-line growth. According to the latest  Earnings Preview, the sector's earnings are projected to increase 7.9% on revenue growth of 8%.

With the assistance of the Zacks Stock Screener, we have identified three utilities, namely Vistra Corp. VST, Ameren Corporation  AEE and PPL Corporation PPL, which are poised to beat on earnings this reporting cycle.

These stocks have the ideal combination of two ingredients — a positive Earnings ESP and a Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy), 2 (Buy), or 3 (Hold) — to surpass expectations. You can uncover the best stocks to buy or sell before they are reported with our  Earnings ESP Filter.

Major Drivers of Utility Stocks' Q1 Performance

Utilities are set to benefit from rising electricity demand, primarily fueled by the rapid expansion of data centers, particularly those supporting AI, along with increased consumption from commercial and industrial customers. In addition, the reshoring of industries amid geopolitical uncertainty has been creating fresh demand for utility services. Collectively, these factors are likely to have supported higher revenues in the upcoming quarter.

Utility service providers continue to benefit from several supportive factors, including higher electricity rates, value-accretive acquisitions, cost-cutting measures and the rollout of energy-efficiency programs. These companies have also been gaining from ongoing investments to strengthen infrastructure against extreme weather, along with a steady transition toward cost-effective renewable energy sources for power generation.

Utilities have been investing in smart meter deployments, expanding transmission and distribution networks and strengthening infrastructure maintenance, all of which enhance operational efficiency and support better customer engagement. These efforts help lower costs, drive revenue growth and improve overall grid management.

At the same time, improving economic conditions across service territories are generating new demand for utility services, supporting stronger revenues and overall performance.

Potential Utility Outperformers for This Earnings Season

Ameren generates and distributes electricity and natural gas to residential, commercial, industrial and wholesale end markets in Missouri and Illinois. The company's systematic investments in growth projects, infrastructure upgrades and renewable portfolio will help improve service reliability and generate higher profits. Ameren plans a major expansion of its clean energy portfolio, targeting the addition of 2,700 MW of renewable generation capacity by 2030 and reaching a total of 4,200 MW by 2035.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for its first-quarter earnings is pegged at $1.17 per share, indicating an increase of 9.35% from the year-ago reported figure. AEE currently has an Earnings ESP of +1.29% and a Zacks Rank #3. You can see the complete list of today's Zacks #1 Rank stocks here.

Ameren Corporation Price and EPS Surprise

Ameren Corporation price-eps-surprise |  Ameren Corporation Quote

Vistra is an integrated power company operating in competitive U.S. markets, supplying electricity and natural gas to residential, commercial and industrial customers, while managing a diversified generation fleet that supports and hedges its retail operations. Long-term contracts with high-quality partners strengthen stability.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for its first-quarter earnings is pegged at $2.21 per share, indicating an increase of 380.43% from the year-ago reported figure. VST currently has an Earnings ESP of +4.79% and a Zacks Rank #3.

Vistra Corp. Price and EPS Surprise

Vistra Corp. price-eps-surprise |  Vistra Corp. Quote

PPL Corporation primarily generates electricity from power plants in the northeastern, northwestern and southeastern United States. The company markets wholesale or retail energy chiefly in northeastern and northwestern portions of the United States. PPL is experiencing load growth, driven by data center demand. In Pennsylvania, nearly 25.2 GW (up from 20.5 GW) of potential data-center demand is in the advanced stages. In the Kentucky segment, the Economic development queue holds total potential load growth of 9.3 GW through 2032 (up from 8.5 GW).

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for its first-quarter earnings is pegged at 0.61 cents per share, indicating an increase of 1.67% from the year-ago reported figure. PPL currently has an Earnings ESP of +0.41% and a Zacks Rank #3.

PPL Corporation Price and EPS Surprise

PPL Corporation price-eps-surprise |  PPL Corporation Quote

Zacks' Research Chief Names "Stock Most Likely to Double"

Our team of experts has just released the 5 stocks with the greatest probability of gaining +100% or more in the coming months. Of those 5, Director of Research Sheraz Mian highlights the one stock set to climb highest.

This top pick is a little-known satellite-based communications firm. Space is projected to become a trillion dollar industry, and this company's customer base is growing fast. May 6th, 2026 (Baseball News Source — Delivered by Newstex)

Shares of REX American Resources Corporation (NYSE:REX -  Get Free Report) hit a new 52-week high during mid-day trading on Tuesday . The stock traded as high as $51.36 and last traded at $51.2160, with a volume of 171574 shares traded. The stock had previously closed at $49.97.

Wall Street Analysts Forecast Growth

Separately, Weiss Ratings lowered REX American Resources from a "buy (b-)" rating to a "hold (c+)" rating in a research note on Monday. One equities research analyst has rated the stock with a Hold rating, According to data from MarketBeat, REX American Resources has an average rating of "Hold".

Read Our Latest Analysis on REX

REX American Resources Stock Performance

The business's fifty day moving average price is $42.40 and its two-hundred day moving average price is $36.43. The stock has a market capitalization of $1.58 billion, a P/E ratio of 19.21 and a beta of 0.63.

REX American Resources (NYSE:REX -  Get Free Report) last released its quarterly earnings results on Thursday, March 26th. The energy company reported $1.32 EPS for the quarter, topping the consensus estimate of $0.14 by $1.18. The company had revenue of $157.96 million during the quarter, compared to analyst estimates of $162.00 million. REX American Resources had a return on equity of 12.72% and a net margin of 12.75%.

Insider Buying and Selling

In other REX American Resources news, CFO Douglas Bruggeman sold 2,000 shares of the business's stock in a transaction on Thursday, April 2nd. The shares were sold at an average price of $45.44, for a total transaction of $90,880.00. Following the transaction, the chief financial officer owned 259,170 shares in the company, valued at $11,776,684.80. The trade was a 0.77% decrease in their ownership of the stock. The transaction was disclosed in a legal filing with the SEC, which is available at  this link. 13.21% of the stock is owned by insiders.

Hedge Funds Weigh In On REX American Resources

Several hedge funds have recently added to or reduced their stakes in the company. Vanguard Group Inc. boosted its position in shares of REX American Resources by 3.8% during the fourth quarter. Vanguard Group Inc. now owns 2,529,944 shares of the energy company's stock valued at $81,768,000 after buying an additional 93,312 shares during the period. Dimensional Fund Advisors LP raised its holdings in shares of REX American Resources by 89.3% in the third quarter. Dimensional Fund Advisors LP now owns 2,152,938 shares of the energy company's stock worth $65,923,000 after buying an additional 1,015,668 shares during the period. Mangrove Partners IM LLC lifted its stake in shares of REX American Resources by 92.8% during the 4th quarter. Mangrove Partners IM LLC now owns 1,015,234 shares of the energy company's stock worth $32,812,000 after acquiring an additional 488,682 shares during the last quarter. American Century Companies Inc. boosted its holdings in REX American Resources by 88.0% during the 3rd quarter. American Century Companies Inc. now owns 826,427 shares of the energy company's stock valued at $25,305,000 after acquiring an additional 386,947 shares during the period. Finally, Geode Capital Management LLC boosted its holdings in REX American Resources by 0.4% during the 4th quarter. Geode Capital Management LLC now owns 695,670 shares of the energy company's stock valued at $22,486,000 after acquiring an additional 2,647 shares during the period. 88.12% of the stock is currently owned by institutional investors.

REX American Resources Company Profile

(Get Free Report)

REX American Resources Corp. is a diversified agribusiness and renewable energy company headquartered in Kansas City, Missouri. Founded in 2005 through a reorganization of existing agricultural interests, the company focuses on two primary business segments: fuel ethanol production and specialty ingredient solutions. REX American Resources leverages its integrated operations to supply clean-burning fuel, animal feed co-products and sweetener ingredients to a broad customer base in North America and beyond.

In its alcohol fuels segment, the company operates an anhydrous ethanol production facility in Colwich, Kansas.
